suggestions.title: STILL UNFIXED: vibeStreak prop never passed to Office — Fern stuck as seedling forever
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1043: <Office ... /> is missing vibeStreak={vibeStreak}. Was flagged in review #4. The fern plant will perpetually render as 'seedling' regardless of how long the team has been crushing it. Zero growth. Fern is sad. Fix: add vibeStreak={vibeStreak} to the Office JSX call in App.jsx.

suggestions.title: STILL UNFIXED: workers.json missing 'roster' key — worker filter bar shows only 'All Workers'
suggestions.description: workers.json has a 'workers' array but no 'roster' array. App.jsx line 682 sets roster=[] when the roster key is absent. The worker filter bar (line 1148) iterates roster.map(...) which is empty, so only the 'All Workers' button ever appears. Fix: add a 'roster' key to workers.json listing the team members.

suggestions.title: Stuck worker card renders literal 'null' — formatDuration called with undefined field
suggestions.description: StatsBar line 457: `formatDuration(stuckWorkers[0].started)`. The field is 'started' but workers.json uses 'updated_at' (and 'startedAt'). formatDuration(undefined) returns null. In the template literal this becomes the visible string 'null': e.g. '🎨 Maya · null'. Any user with a stuck worker will see this right now. Fix: change .started to .updated_at (or .startedAt) in StatsBar.

suggestions.title: Mobile: massive blank space below office floor, office canvas tiny and unreadable
suggestions.description: On 375px wide viewport, the office floor has aspect-ratio: 4/3 and max-width: 900px. It renders at ~330px × ~248px — barely big enough to read worker names. Whiteboard text ('SHIP IT') overlaps the leftmost desk column. Below the floor there is ~400px of dead space before the footer. Fix: on mobile, either increase the floor's min-height, add a scrollable/pannable canvas, or adopt a stacked list view for the office when viewport < 480px.

suggestions.title: React.memo on WorkerCard defeated every 30s — activityEntries is a new array ref each sync
suggestions.description: App.jsx sets state via setActivityLog(Array.isArray(activity) ? activity : []) on every sync, creating a new array reference. This reference is passed directly as activityEntries to every WorkerCard. Since React.memo does shallow comparison, ALL WorkerCards re-render on every 30s poll even if worker data didn't change. Fix: memoize activityEntries or filter it per-worker before passing, so stable references are preserved.

suggestions.title: ShortcutToast timer leak on 'Got it' path — first setTimeout ref silently orphaned
suggestions.description: ShortcutToast lines 527–531: when gotIt=true, timerRef.current is assigned at line 527 (setPhase fading-out after 500ms) then immediately overwritten at line 531 (setPhase hidden after 900ms). The cleanup effect (line 555) can only cancel the second timer. The first orphaned timer fires after unmount, calling setPhase on an unmounted component. Fix: use separate refs for the two timers, or calculate total delay and use a single setTimeout.

suggestions.title: VibeSparkline always lags one entry — child useEffect reads localStorage before parent writes it
suggestions.description: React runs child effects before parent effects in the same commit. StatsBar's useEffect (vibe.key dep) reads vibe history from localStorage before App.jsx's useEffect (teamVibe.key dep) has written the current entry. The sparkline always shows history minus the current vibe change. Fix: lift the vibe history state up into App.jsx, compute it alongside the streak, and pass it down as a prop to StatsBar/VibeSparkline.

suggestions.title: Worker hover card displays raw status string — STATUS_LABELS not used
suggestions.description:
Character component (Office.jsx line 482) renders `
{worker.status}
` — shows lowercase 'working', 'idle', 'error' instead of the friendly labelled versions. STATUS_LABELS and STATUS_EMOJIS already exist in constants.js for exactly this purpose. Fix: replace `{worker.status}` with `{STATUS_EMOJIS[worker.status]} {STATUS_LABELS[worker.status]}`.

suggestions.title: ETA calculation uses updated_at (sync timestamp) not actual task start — wildly inaccurate
suggestions.description: App.jsx ProgressBar line 193: workingMs is computed from worker.updated_at. This field reflects the last data sync timestamp, NOT when the worker started their task. workers.json already has a 'startedAt' field that's never used. A task that was 90% complete when the page loaded will show a tiny ETA because updated_at is recent. Fix: use worker.startedAt for ETA calculation. Also pass it through from the data model.

suggestions.title: Fading WorkerCards all get index={activeWorkers.length} — identical stagger delay for all
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1102: `index={activeWorkers.length}` is hardcoded for every fading worker card. This means when multiple workers depart simultaneously, all fading cards get the same CSS --i variable and animate in lockstep instead of staggering. Fix: use a separate counter variable or the worker's original index from activeWorkers.

suggestions.title: STILL UNFIXED: completionToast uses 12 inline style declarations — CSS class pending
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 942–962: completionToast renders with a large inline style object (position, bottom, left, transform, background, border, borderRadius, padding, fontSize, fontWeight, color, boxShadow, zIndex, whiteSpace, pointerEvents, animation). Was flagged review #4 as low priority conversion to CSS class. Still there. Fix: move to a .completion-toast class in App.css and use the border color as a CSS custom property.
